“The best and most beautiful things in the world cannot be seen or even touched—they must be felt with the heart.” —Helen Keller
“To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all.” —Oscar Wilde
“The purpose of life is to live it, to taste experience to the utmost, to reach out eagerly and without fear for newer and richer experience.” —Eleanor Roosevelt
“Happiness is not a matter of intensity but of balance, order, rhythm and harmony.” —Thomas Merton
“I will not follow where the path may lead, but I will go where there is no path, and I will leave a trail.” —Muriel Strode, The Open Court
“Do not let making a living prevent you from making a life.” —John Wooden
“The beauty of life is in small details, not in big events.” —Jim Jarmusch
“The beauty of life is in small details, not in big events.” —Jim Jarmusch
“To succeed in life, you need three things: a wishbone, a backbone and a funny bone.” —Dr. Kavanagh
“Life is what happens when you’re busy making other plans.” —John Lennon, Beautiful Boy
“Happiness is not something ready-made. It comes from your own actions.” —Dalai Lama
“You have within you, right now, everything you need to deal with whatever the world can throw at you.” —Brian Tracy
“Life isn’t about finding yourself. Life is about creating yourself.” —George Bernard Shaw
“The more you praise and celebrate your life, the more there is in life to celebrate.” —Oprah Winfrey
“The meaning of life is to give life meaning.” —Viktor E. Frankl
“Life is short, and it is up to you to make it sweet.” —Sarah Louise Delany
“Life is 10% what happens to us and 90% how we react to it.” —Charles R. Swindoll
“Life is made of ever so many partings welded together.” —Charles Dickens
“If you love life, don’t waste time, for time is what life is made up of.” —Bruce Lee
“Never let the fear of striking out keep you from playing the game.” —Babe Ruth
“Life is short, and it is here to be lived.” —Kate Winslet
“Life isn’t about waiting for the storm to pass, it’s about learning to dance in the rain.” —Vivian Greene
“So be sure when you step. Step with care and great tact; and remember that Life’s a Great Balancing Act.” —Dr. Seuss, Oh, the Places You’ll Go
“I believe every human has a finite number of heartbeats. I don’t intend to waste any of mine.” —Neil Armstrong
“Life is 10% what happens to us and 90% how we react to it.”—Charles R. Swindoll
Life Is Beautiful Quotes
Life’s beauty can be found in both small moments and grand experiences. These quotes remind us to pause and appreciate the simple wonders around us.
“Life is beautiful. It’s about giving. It’s about family.” —Walt Disney
“I enjoy being happy every day, and hopefully you can hear my happiness in my music. Life is beautiful.” —Christina Milian
“A man who dares to waste one hour of time has not discovered the value of life.”—Charles Darwin
“The purpose of our lives is to be happy.” —Dalai Lama
“Life is beautiful, live it as if it were the last day of your life.” —Unattributed
“Enjoy the little things, for one day you may look back and realize they were the big things.” —Robert Brault
“Every day is a good day. There is something to learn, care and celebrate.” —Amit Ray
“Life is full of beauty. Notice it. Notice the bumblebee, the small child, and the smiling faces. Smell the rain, and feel the wind.” —Ashley Smith
“Life is beautiful, and there’s so much to smile about.” —Marilyn Monroe
“Life is beautiful, and there’s so much to smile about.” —Marilyn Monroe
“When you arise in the morning, think of what a privilege it is to be alive, to think, to enjoy, to love.” —Marcus Aurelius
“To be beautiful means to be yourself. You don’t need to be accepted by others. You need to accept yourself.” —Thich Nhat Hanh
“What a wonderful thought it is that some of the best days of our lives haven’t happened yet.” —Anne Frank
“Life is beautiful, but people are crazy.” —Charles Osgood
“Life is beautiful, what do you think? In the morning I say, ‘Ah, I am alive still!’ All my friends die already. I am alive. It is fantastic.” —Alejandro Jodorowsky
“To me, life is a gift, and it’s a blessing to just be alive.” —Tony Bennett
“Even in a world with much sadness, at its essence, life is beautiful.” —Dianne Reeves
“Even in a world with much sadness, at its essence, life is beautiful.” —Dianne Reeves
“Look at the world with the child’s eye—it is very beautiful.” —Kailash Satyarthi
“Life is beautiful because it doesn’t last.” —Brit Marling
“Life is beautiful not because of the things we see or do. Life is beautiful because of the people we meet.” —Simon Sinek
“Relax! Life is beautiful!” —David L. Wolper

Quotes About The Beauty Of Life
These quotes reflect on the beauty of life in a more philosophical sense, inviting us to seek meaning and joy even in the smallest moments.
“Find ecstasy in life; the mere sense of living is joy enough.” —Emily Dickinson

“There is nothing more beautiful than finding your course as you believe you bob aimlessly in the current. Wouldn’t you know that your path was there all along, waiting for you to find it?” —Jodie Foster
“Life is a series of natural and spontaneous changes. Don’t resist them; that only creates sorrow. Let reality be reality. Let things flow naturally forward in whatever way they like.” —Lao Tzu
“Beauty is not in the face; beauty is a light in the heart.” —Kahlil Gibran
“You’re only here for a short visit. Don’t hurry, don’t worry. And be sure to smell the flowers along the way.” —Walter Hagen
“Think of all the beauty still left around you and be happy.” —Anne Frank
“The longer I live, the more deeply I learn that love—whether we call it friendship or family or romance—is the work of mirroring and magnifying each other’s light.” —James  Baldwin
“Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.” —Marcus Aurelius
“Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.” —Marcus Aurelius
“No one is you, and that is your power.” —Dave Grohl
“This is the real secret of life—to be completely engaged with what you are doing in the here and now. And instead of calling it work, realize it is play.” —Alan Watts
“If you want to be happy, be. ” —Leo Tolstoy
“Life is full of beauty. Notice it in the bumblebee, the small child and the smiling faces.” —Ashley Smith
“A life is beautiful and ideal or the reverse, only when we have taken into our consideration the social as well as the family relationship.” —Havelock Ellis
“Sometimes you will never know the value of a moment until it becomes a memory.” —Dr. Seuss
“Taking joy in living is a woman’s best cosmetic.” —Rosalind Russell
“There is beauty in everything; even in silence and darkness.” —Helen Keller
“There is beauty in everything; even in silence and darkness.” —Helen Keller
“There is nothing more beautiful than seeing a person being themselves.” —Steve Maraboli
“Let the beauty of what you love be what you do. There are a thousand ways to kneel and kiss the earth.” —Rumi
“Embrace uncertainty. Some of the most beautiful chapters in our lives won’t have a title until much later.” —Bob Goff
“It’s the possibility of having a dream come true that makes life interesting.” —Paulo Coelho
“Beauty is how you feel inside, and it reflects in your eyes. It is not something physical.” —Sophia Loren

Inspiring Beautiful Quotes
Some of the most inspiring quotes offer us not only beauty, but also a sense of motivation to live fully.
“Keep your face always toward the sunshine—and shadows will fall behind you.” —Walt Whitman
“The best way to predict the future is to create it.” —Peter Drucker
“Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.” —William James
“We always may be what we might have been.” —Adelaide Anne Procter, The Ghost in the Picture Room
“Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one’s courage.” —Anaïs Nin
“We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give.” —Winston Churchill
“Optimism is a happiness magnet. If you stay positive, good things and good people will be drawn to you.” —Mary Lou Retton
“Optimism is a happiness magnet. If you stay positive, good things and good people will be drawn to you.” —Mary Lou Retton
“The only way to do great work is to love what you do.” —Steve Jobs
“When you have a dream, you’ve got to grab it and never let go.” —Carol Burnett
“Don’t wait for extraordinary opportunities. Seize common occasions and make them great.” —Orison Swett Marden
“If you risk nothing, then you risk everything.” —Geena Davis
“Your life does not get better by chance, it gets better by change.” —Jim Rohn
“You are never too small to make a difference.” —Greta Thunberg
“What you do today can improve all your tomorrows.” —Ralph Marston
“In the end, we only regret the chances we didn’t take.” —Lewis Carroll
“In the end, we only regret the chances we didn’t take.” —Lewis Carroll
“Don’t sit down and wait for the opportunities to come. Get up and make them.” —Madam C.J. Walker
“Every day may not be good, but there’s something good in every day.” —Alice Morse Earle
“In a gentle way, you can shake the world.” —Mahatma Gandhi
“I have found that if you love life, life will love you back.” —Arthur Rubinstein
“Love the life you live. Live the life you love.” —Bob Marley
“Life is a journey, and if you fall in love with the journey, you will be in love forever.” —Peter Hagerty
Famous Beautiful Quotes
These famous quotes by great minds and leaders have stood the test of time, offering timeless wisdom about the beauty of life.
“The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.” —Mahatma Gandhi
“Happiness will never come to those who fail to appreciate that which they already have.” —Buddha
“The only thing we have to fear is fear itself.” —Franklin D. Roosevelt
“In three words, I can sum up everything I’ve learned about life: It goes on.” —Robert Frost
“The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.”—Lao Tzu
“The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.” —Lao Tzu
“A thing of beauty is a joy forever: Its loveliness increases; it will never Pass into nothingness…” —John Keats
“It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.” —Confucius
“Happiness depends upon ourselves.” —Aristotle
“The unexamined life is not worth living.” —Socrates
“The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.” —Eleanor Roosevelt
“The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.” —Eleanor Roosevelt
“Success is not the key to happiness. Happiness is the key to success. If you love what you are doing, you will be successful.” —Albert Schweitzer
“Definitions belong to the definers, not the defined.” —Toni Morrison
“The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.” —Nelson Mandela
“Champions keep playing until they get it right.” —Billie Jean King
